title: salix hastata subsp. hastatella
intro: Salix hastata subsp. hastatella is a subspecies of willow tree, scientifically known as a type of shrub or small tree belonging to the Salicaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of pain and inflammation due to its salicylic acid content. Salix hastata subsp. hastatella has been found to offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can aid in reducing fever, soothing wounds, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
